dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - April 23, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**Braves Take Down Nationals Behind Albies' Big Night**

Ozzie Albies led the Atlanta Braves to a 7-2 victory over the Washington Nationals, homering for the fifth time this season and driving in four runs. Albies finished 3-for-4 with the long ball, a highlight of a strong team effort that saw Matt Olson and Michael Harris II also contribute with multi-hit games. On the mound, JR Ritchie earned the win with a solid outing. In the Nationals' loss, Cade Cavalli turned in a strong performance, striking out 10 over five innings.

**Tigers Stun Brewers in Extra Innings**

The Detroit Tigers edged the Milwaukee Brewers 5-4 in a back-and-forth contest, with Riley Greene delivering a crucial home run and two RBIs. Spencer Torkelson also went deep for the Tigers, while Gary Sánchez and David Hamilton contributed with multi-hit games for the Brewers. Grant Anderson earned the win in relief for the Tigers.

**Cubs Outlast Phillies in Thrilling Finish**

The Chicago Cubs pulled off a 7-8 comeback victory over the Philadelphia Phillies, with Brandon Marsh hitting two home runs and driving in three runs for the Phillies. Dansby Swanson's late-game single proved to be the difference-maker, scoring Ian Happ and setting up the walk-off win for the Cubs. Javier Assad earned the win in relief.

**Padres Outslug Rockies in High-Scoring Affair**

The San Diego Padres scored 10 runs to top the Colorado Rockies 10-8, with Mickey Moniak hitting two home runs and driving in two runs. Gavin Sheets also went deep for the Padres, while Ramón Laureano and Manny Machado each contributed with multi-hit games. Ron Marinaccio earned the win for the Padres.

**White Sox Blank Diamondbacks**

The Chicago White Sox cruised to a 4-1 victory over the Arizona Diamondbacks, with Andrew Benintendi hitting a solo home run and driving in three runs. Davis Martin turned in a strong outing on the mound, striking out seven over six innings. Grant Taylor earned the win for the White Sox.

**Dodgers Shut Down Giants**

The Los Angeles Dodgers dominated the San Francisco Giants 3-0, with Tyler Glasnow pitching a complete-game shutout. Glasnow struck out nine over eight innings, allowing just one hit. Hyeseong Kim contributed with a multi-hit game for the Dodgers.

**Yankees Edge Red Sox**

The New York Yankees topped the Boston Red Sox 4-2, with Payton Tolle turning in a strong outing on the mound, striking out 11 over six innings. Jazz Chisholm Jr. hit a solo home run for the Yankees. Cam Schlittler earned the win in relief.

**Mets Outlast Twins**

The New York Mets edged the Minnesota Twins 10-8, with Ryan Jeffers hitting a solo home run and driving in four runs for the Twins. Travis Gray also contributed with a late-game single, scoring Brooks Lee. Devin Williams earned the win in relief for the Mets.

**Rangers Rout Pirates**

The Texas Rangers cruised to a 6-1 victory over the Pittsburgh Pirates, with Corey Seager hitting a solo home run and driving in three runs. Jacob deGrom turned in a strong outing on the mound, striking out 10 over five innings.
